page: Microsoft Azure G1 is a G-family virtual machine instance powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server features 2 dedicated physical cores and 2 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 28.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 14.0 GB per core. Additionally, the instance includes 393 GB of bundled local storage, while containing no GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the G1 instance is optimized primarily for memory capacity rather than raw processing speed. CPU-bound benchmarks, such as stress-ng single-core and multi-core tests, place the instance in the bottom 10% of performance. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are categorized as weak and poor, respectively. Memory bandwidth tests for large blocks also register in the bottom 10%, though single-threaded gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation achieve average tier results.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the G1 instance offers a high memory footprint relative to its compute resources, making it suitable for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high CPU throughput. However, the older Intel Xeon architecture presents a clear performance tradeoff, as demonstrated by low scores in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving. This server is best suited for legacy enterprise applications, small-scale databases, caching layers, or memory-heavy utility tasks with modest CPU demands.
description: Microsoft Azure G1 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2698B v3 processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 28.0 GB of RAM, providing a high allocation of 14.0 GB per core, and includes 393 GB of bundled local storage. Benchmarks reveal a clear tradeoff: while the instance offers substantial memory capacity, its compute performance is limited, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores falling into the bottom 10% to 25% ranges. Gzip compression and small-model LLM text generation perform at average levels. Given this profile, the G1 instance is qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy workloads with low CPU demands. It is best suited for legacy enterprise applications, small databases, caching layers, and basic memory-intensive utility tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure G1 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 2 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 28.0 GB of RAM, and 393 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive workloads with modest compute requirements.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine offering high RAM-to-core density and bundled local storage for workloads with modest processing requirements.
